note: Channel is 12 days old (started 2026-06-09). 30 videos total, 29 matured retention, 1 pending (re-published czechia-south-africa from 2026-06-20). 9 new retention records since 2026-06-18: the 8 previously-pending videos (france-senegal 55.08%, norway-iraq 102.75%, argentina-algeria 62.89%, austria-jordan 53.46%, portugal-dr-congo 58.9%, england-croatia 52.76%, ghana-panama 54.75%, colombia-uzbekistan 49.5%) plus new czechia-south-africa (53.15%). KEY FINDING: norway-iraq (Haaland, 102.75% avgView — second video to exceed 100% after messi-last-dance) confirms the single-subject-specific-deprivation pattern is repeatable, not a fluke. Both use a specific quantified fact about the SUBJECT's failure to score at a World Cup. BOTTOM OF THE TABLE: colombia-uzbekistan (49.5%), korea-czechia (48.42%), netherlands-japan-preview (48.13%) — all leak at scene 1-2 with hooks that either hand the story to the opponent or use an abstract frame. Baseline stable at ~59% median avg_view_pct, ~0.15 retained_to_end. Subscribe conversion stuck at 0.11% — unchanged for 9 days despite adding 30 more videos. First mid-roll subscribe-ask data point: czechia-south-africa put a subscribe ask at scene 3 and got 0 subs + that scene was the leak point.

learnings.finding: Overall subscribe conversion is 0.11% (59 subs from ~53k total views). Unchanged for 9 days despite growing from 8 to 30 videos. First mid-roll subscribe-ask data point: czechia-south-africa put a subscribe ask at scene 3 and got 0 subs — and that scene was the biggest retention drop. The end-card-only subscribe scenes also fail consistently: germany-curacao (0 subs), belgium-egypt (0 subs), sweden-tunisia (no subscribe scene, 6.3k views). The 13.3k-view outlier (netherlands-japan-comeback) is the only video that drove meaningful subs (13) from raw reach, not from the subscribe ask.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Subscribe conversion is the channel's biggest vulnerability — 0.11% is very low. The end-card and mid-roll subscribe asks both fail. Try a direct, mid-roll subscribe ask that ties to the video's subject ('subscribe to follow [player]'s World Cup journey'), or a visual subscribe countdown overlay instead of a dedicated scene. Consider a subscribe-themed hook as the LAST beat of the body (not a separate scene).

learnings.finding: post_match format still dominates reach: netherlands-japan-comeback 13.3k, sweden-tunisia 6.3k, ivory-coast 2.1k, iran-nz 1.8k, argentina-algeria 1.7k, norway-iraq 1.5k. Median post_match avg_view_pct among 13 matured is ~57% — below the channel median of 59%. New data: ghana-panama (54.75%), czechia-south-africa (53.15%), france-senegal (55.08%), austria-jordan (53.46%) all below median. The spain-cabo-verde outlier (84.52%, framed as giant's failure) and norway-iraq player_story (102.75%) confirm the driver is NARRATIVE FRAME, not format label.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Keep post_match as the reach driver but frame EVERY match around ONE person's failure/redemption or the underdog's moment, never a chronological timeline. The Cabo Verde/Haaland templates prove this works. Target <=28s. Always set has_subscribe_scene (even though it barely converts yet).

learnings.finding: 12 of 29 matured videos (41%) have their biggest retention drop at scene 2 — the first body beat after the hook. Down from 50% (10/20) at last analysis but still the #1 leak point. Videos that ESCAPE scene-2 leakage are the elite holders: norway-iraq (102.75%, leak at scene 6 group_table), messi-last-dance (100.68%, scene 1), caboverde (84.57%, scene 5), germany-curacao (81.06%, scene 1). Videos that leak at scene 2: avg ~55-57%. New confirms: france-senegal (55.08%, scene 2 scorers_split — opens with '2002: SENEGAL 1-0 FRANCE', a historical opponent moment), ghana-panama (54.75%, scene 2 stat_card — 'PARTEY OUT — 20YO IN', a downgrade narrative), austria-jordan (53.46%, scene 2 — 'SCHMID. 20'. 1-0.', a chronological timeline open).
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Scene 2 is still the single biggest retention lever. Never open scene 2 with the opponent scoring, a downgrade narrative, or a chronological moment. Every first body beat must BUILD on the hook's stakes. The Haaland video proves the pattern: scene 2 = '28 YEARS. ONE STAGE.' — escalates the hook about Haaland's WC drought.

learnings.finding: The best HOLDERS are now definitively single-subject rising arcs with a SPECIFIC, QUANTIFIED deprivation about the subject: norway-iraq (102.75%, '55 goals. Zero at a World Cup.'), messi-last-dance (100.68%, 'record sixth World Cup'), germany-curacao (80.16%, 'smallest nation ever scored'), caboverde-spain (84.52%, '90 minutes. Zero goals.' — framed as Spain's failure). The worst holders are multi-beat match narratives or videos with abstract hooks: colombia-uzbekistan (49.5%, abstract 'pass that changed it'), korea-czechia (48.42%, opponent-first hook), netherlands-japan-preview (48.13%, opponent-first hook). england-croatia (52.76%) is instructive: it's a player_story (Bellingham) but the hook is about his PRICE TAG ('200 million'), not a performance deprivation, and scene 2 hands the story to Croatia. Even the player_story format fails if the execution is wrong.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: The strongest pattern is quantified deprivation about the subject in the hook. Frame every story as one person's specific shortcoming/record chase. Never open on the opponent's achievement. Pick the surprise hero (not the obvious star). The Haaland and Messi videos are the templates.

learnings.finding: post_match format still nearly silent: most new post_match videos have 0 comments. The few with comments use a contestable, specific debate question: england-croatia (5 comments, 'Croatia or Ghana — who finishes 2nd?'), portugal-dr-congo (4 comments, 'Who's winning Group K now?'), pulisic (8 comments, verdict prompt). uruguay-saudi remains the outlier (11 comments) with its group-debate question. The mechanic is provocation (contestable, specific, group-debate framing), not format.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Never end with a yes/no or soft question. Use a contestable, specific debate: name the teams, ask for a pick, frame a disagreement. The uruguay-saudi, england-croatia, and portugal-dr-congo videos all prove the mechanic works when the prompt is contestable.

learnings.finding: With 29 matured videos, the pattern is definitive: best hook survival on videos with a SPECIFIC, QUANTIFIED deprivation about the SUBJECT. norway-iraq ('55 goals. Zero at a World Cup.' — 102.75% avgView) joins messi-last-dance ('His sixth World Cup' — 100.68%) as double proof. pulisic ('27 games without a goal' — 58.94%) and balogun ('nobody trusted USA's striker' — 71.41%) follow the same pattern. Worst: hooks opening on the opponent's achievement or abstract frames: netherlands-japan-preview ('JAPAN BEAT ENGLAND', 48.13%), korea-czechia ('SON CAME OFF — THEN THIS', 48.42%), colombia-uzbekistan ('27 YEARS. ZERO WC GOALS. TONIGHT.' — actually a good hook but scene 2 abstract fails it, 49.5%).
learnings.proposed_rule_change: First frame = a specific, quantified, unresolved fact about the SUBJECT (or the giant's quantified failure). Never open on the opponent's goal or achievement. Scene 2 must escalate (not deflate) the hook. The Haaland/Messi model is proven repeatable.

learnings.finding: The gap between best and worst holders is 55 percentage points (norway-iraq 102.75% vs colombia-uzbekistan 49.5%). Within the SAME format (player_story), norway-iraq holds 102.75% and england-croatia holds 52.76%. Within post_match, caboverde holds 84.52% and czechia-south-africa holds 53.15%. The driver is execution — specifically whether the hook quantifies a specific deprivation and scene 2 escalates it.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Do not trust format to carry retention. Audit every scene 2: does it escalate the hook or hand the story to the opponent? Hook must be a specific, quantified deprivation about the subject. Build a tight rise through the first 3 scenes around ONE person's arc.

learnings.finding: TWO leak stages confirmed: (1) impression-level swipe-away (estimated ~60-70%, immeasurable from Analytics API). (2) AMONG counted views past the start: scene 2 is still the #1 drop (41% of videos), then a gradual body bleed. Retained_to_end ranges 0.080-0.401 (median 0.15). New data: 3 videos now leak at scene 6 (group_table) — norway-iraq, portugal-dr-congo, netherlands-japan-comeback — and 1 at scene 5 (group_table — spain-cabo-verde). These are the highest-retention videos where group_table appears; the group_table leak may be a 'video is over' signal but these viewers had already watched most of the video.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Fix the body: scene 2 is the single biggest lever — must escalate the hook, never deflate it. For group_table scenes at positions 5-6: consider adding a re-engagement line in the VO before the table, or moving the subscribe prompt to after the table with a reason to keep watching.

learnings.finding: quiz_top5 remains the worst-reaching format by far: mean ~381 views vs ~1.3k+ for post_match recaps. Quiz retention is mid-pack (avg 63.57-63.59%) but the reach gap is 3-4x. No new quiz data since the first two — the reach problem is format-level, not execution.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Keep quiz as rare seasoning only. Graft the quiz comment-engine mechanic (contestable question) onto narrative formats instead of making quiz videos.

learnings.finding: All new videos are 21-30s rendered duration (none exceed 30s). The post_match format that drives the most reach reliably stays <=30s. The 56s quizzes ($\sim$400 views) vs \~28s post_match ($\sim$1.3k views) gap is format-confounded but staying short correlates with every high-reach video. Evidence now at n=22.

Pattern continues: all new post_match videos name both countries in the title with the scoreline. The reach floor remains the non-country-naming quizzes (355, 408). Still confounded with format but costs nothing and correlates with every high-reach video.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: For any match video, put BOTH nations' names in the youtube_title (front-loaded with the scoreline).

learnings.finding: First data point on mid-roll subscribe asks: czechia-south-africa put a dedicated subscribe scene at position 3 ('SUBSCRIBE — FOLLOW BAFANA BAFANA') and got 0 subscribers — and that scene (3) was the biggest retention leak. N=1, too early for confidence, but the end-card subscribe asks also convert at ~0%.
learnings.proposed_rule_change: Do not use a mid-roll subscribe scene as a standalone beat; it leaks. If trying mid-roll subscribe, overlay it on a narrative beat instead of a dedicated scene.

learnings.finding: SUPERSEDED by comment-conversion-needs-provocation-not-format (the mechanic is provocation, not quiz format). The uruguay-saudi post_match (11 comments), england-croatia player_story (5 comments), and portugal-dr-congo player_story (4 comments) all prove the same contestable-question mechanic works on narrative formats.
SUPERSEDED by retention-is-execution-not-format and single-subject-specific-deprivation-elite-hold. Execution drives retention, not format label.
